questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the given sentence. The esoteric nature of the philosopher's ideas made them difficult for the general public to understand.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is Complex. The word esoteric refers to something that is understood by only a small, specialized group of people due to its complexity or obscurity. Therefore, the synonym that aligns closely with this meaning is complex. The other options: • Common: Opposite in meaning to esoteric, as it suggests something easily understood or widely known. • Simple: Also opposite to esoteric, implying something straightforward or easy to grasp. • Popular: Refers to being widely liked or accepted, not related to the exclusivity or difficulty implied by esoteric.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Catch a tartar
options: To find something delicious and satisfying
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To meet someone who is friendly and welcoming
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To unexpectedly find a person who is more difficult to deal with than anticipated
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To capture a rare or valuable object
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: To unexpectedly find a person who is more difficult to deal with than anticipated. The idiom "catch a tartar" means to encounter someone who is unexpectedly strong, aggressive, or challenging to handle. The phrase often implies underestimating the difficulty of dealing with that person or situation. The other options: • To find something delicious and satisfying: Not related to the idiom. • To meet someone who is friendly and welcoming: Opposite in tone, as "catch a tartar" describes a challenging encounter. • To capture a rare or valuable object: Incorrect, as it doesn't match the idiom's intent.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. A diplomatic representative in another country
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Correct answer: - Ambassador An ambassador is a high-ranking official who represents their country in another nation. They serve as diplomatic representatives and work to maintain good relations, negotiate agreements, and promote their home country's interests abroad. The other options: • Consul: A government official who handles administrative matters like visas and trade in a foreign city, but not at the diplomatic level of an ambassador. • Secretary: A general term for someone assisting in administrative work, not necessarily linked to diplomacy. • Bureaucrat: Refers to an official in a government department, not specific to international relations or diplomacy.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. An assembly of listeners
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: Audience An audience refers specifically to a group of people gathered to listen to a performance, lecture, or event, making it the correct choice. While crowd and spectators can refer to groups of people in general, they don’t necessarily imply that they are listeners. Group is too general and doesn't specify listeners.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. One who takes pleasure in eating and drinking
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Correct Answer: Epicurean Explanation: An Epicurean is someone who takes great pleasure in fine food and drink, often with a focus on luxury and enjoyment. The other options: • Teetotaller: Refers to someone who abstains from alcohol, which is unrelated to indulgence in eating and drinking. • Glutton: Refers to someone who overeats excessively, which carries a negative connotation, unlike "Epicurean." Vegetarian: Describes someone who doesn't eat meat, not related to the enjoyment of food and drink.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. One who goes on a journey to a holy place.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Correct Answer: Pilgrim Explanation: A pilgrim is someone who goes on a journey to a sacred or holy place, often for religious purposes. • A tourist is someone who travels for leisure or recreation. • An explorer is someone who travels to discover new places, typically with a focus on exploration. An adventurer is someone who seeks exciting or unusual experiences, often involving risk.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. (A) It is very cold here/ (B) I should bring/ (C) my woollen clothes but I didn't./ (D) No error.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b The sentence discusses a past situation, as indicated by "but I didn't." The phrase "I should bring" is in the present tense and does not align with the past context. To correct it, "I should bring" should be changed to "I should have brought" to match the past tense.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A collection of wild animals that are kept privately or to show to the public
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is Menagerie. A menagerie is a collection of wild animals kept in captivity for display to the public or maintained privately. This term is specifically used for such a group of animals. The other options: • Vignettes: Refers to brief, descriptive episodes or scenes, not related to animals. • Stallion: Refers to a male horse, not a collection of animals. • Claque: Refers to a group of people hired to applaud, unrelated to animals.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence. Her response was d̲e̲l̲i̲b̲e̲r̲a̲t̲e̲, carefully thought out before speaking.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is Spontaneous. The word deliberate means something done intentionally and with careful thought. Its antonym is spontaneous, which refers to actions done without planning or forethought, often impulsively. The other options: • Planned: Synonymous with deliberate, not an antonym. • Calculated: Similar to deliberate, as it implies premeditation. • Intentional: Also a synonym of deliberate, so it is not the opposite.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the sentence that does NOT have a spelling error.
options: Carbon intensity is a useful way to measure how much carbon a particular sector is emiting and how it has increased or decreased over time.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Experts have previously warned that if the dry weather continued, it could lead to a drought-like situation in the subsequent spring and summer.

*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Earlier, Smith and Alex Carey provided a good platform for Australia to post a big total. However, a disciplened bowling performance and proactive captaincy from Rohit helped India.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Firefly Aerospace suceeded in its first attempt to land on the moon with its uncrewed Blue Ghost spacecraft on Sunday.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b The correct sentence is: "Experts have previously warned that if the dry weather continued, it could lead to a drought-like situation in the subsequent spring and summer." Explanation: This sentence does not have any spelling errors. The remaining sentences contain the following mistakes: • "emiting": The correct spelling is "emitting". • "disciplened": The correct spelling is "disciplined" . • "suceeded": The correct spelling is "succeeded".

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Archaeologists in China have found the remains of what may be the world’s oldest known flush toilet. B. The excavation team said the toilet would have likely been reserved for high-ranking officials during the Han Dynasty (206 BC – 220 AD). C. The toilet was reportedly discovered in the ruins of a palace. D. Broken parts of the 2400-year-old lavatory and a bent flush pipe were unearthed at an archaeological site in the Chinese city of Xi’an.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: c Explanation: • A: Introduces the main topic—archaeologists finding the remains of an ancient flush toilet. • D: Provides details about the discovery—broken parts and a bent flush pipe were found in Xi’an. • C: Adds context to the location—the toilet was found in the ruins of a palace. • B: Concludes by explaining its significance—the toilet was likely used by high-ranking officials during the Han Dynasty.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in bold. The island frustrated Wallace, who repeatedly redrew his line because he was unsure whether it belonged to Asia or Australia.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: a Explanation: Frustrated means feeling upset or defeated because of being unable to achieve something or resolve a problem. Elated means extremely happy or overjoyed, which is the opposite of feeling frustrated. Confused can be a result of frustration, but it’s not a direct antonym. Agitated implies a state of nervousness or excitement, which can still be linked to frustration but doesn’t serve as its exact opposite. Disappointed is similar in meaning to frustration but doesn’t directly oppose it.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. Her optimistic attitude always brightened the room.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is Pessimistic. The word optimistic means having a positive and hopeful outlook on life. Its antonym is pessimistic, which refers to a negative or gloomy outlook. The other options: • Hopeful: Similar in meaning to optimistic, not an antonym. • Positive: Also similar in meaning to optimistic, not an antonym. • Cheerful: Related to happiness and positivity, so it's not the opposite of optimistic.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the given sentence. The Indian AISI is already seeking to advance indigenuous research and development, leveraging Indian datasets.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: c Explanation: The word "indigenuous" is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is "indigenous", which refers to something originating or occurring naturally in a specific place.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Deception
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is Truth. Explanation: The word Deception means the act of deceiving or misleading someone. Its antonym is Truth, which refers to honesty or being genuine. The other options like Fraud, Lie, and Trickery are synonyms or related to deception, not opposites.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Was the room being cleaned by the housekeeper?
options: Did the housekeeper cleaning the room?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Was the housekeeper cleaning the room?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Did the housekeeper clean the room?
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Had the room been cleaned by the housekeeper?
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: In the active voice, the subject (housekeeper) performs the action (cleaning) directly on the object (room). The passive voice, "Was the room being cleaned by the housekeeper?", focuses on the room being cleaned, whereas in active voice, the focus is on the person performing the action.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the given question, a statement divided into different segments is given. Rearrange all the segments to form a coherent statement. P: Who help ensure safe and efficient air travel Q: It is one of the most crucial systems in modern-day aviation, as without it, there would be chaos in air travel R: It is responsible for directing planes during take-off, landing and in-flight communication, preventing any kind of crashes or collisions S: Air traffic control (ATC) is a service provided by ground-based air traffic controllers
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: d Explanation: S: Air traffic control (ATC) is a service provided by ground-based air traffic controllers P: Who help ensure safe and efficient air travel R: It is responsible for directing planes during takeoff, landing and in-flight communication, preventing any kind of crashes or collisions Q: It is one of the most crucial systems in modern-day aviation, as without it, there would be chaos in air travel

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Fill in the blank with the correct part of speech: I like both tea ______ coffee. You can make either.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: c Explanation: The word and is used to connect two elements that are both included or jointly considered. In the sentence, "I like both tea and coffee," the word and links tea and coffee to indicate that the speaker enjoys both options.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Beavers are large, semi-aquatic rodents (1)______ in North America and Europe, known for their sharp teeth and powerful tails. They are expert engineers, building dams (2)_______rivers to create deep ponds for protection. Using branches, mud, and stones, they (3)______ lodges with underwater entrances, keeping predators away. These dams also help control flooding and (4)______wetlands that support various species. Beavers' building skills shape entire ecosystems, making them one of nature’s (5)______influential beings. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b The sentence is describing where beavers are commonly located, specifically in North America and Europe. The word "found" is used to indicate the places where beavers are naturally located or exist. "Finds" is a present tense verb, which doesn’t fit because the sentence is describing something about beavers’ natural habitat. "Existed" is in the past tense, which doesn’t align with the current existence of beavers in these regions. "Discovered" refers to something being found for the first time, but this doesn’t make sense here since beavers have been known to inhabit these areas for a long time. Therefore, "found" is the correct choice as it refers to the current natural distribution of beavers in these regions.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Beavers are large, semi-aquatic rodents (1)______ in North America and Europe, known for their sharp teeth and powerful tails. They are expert engineers, building dams (2)_______rivers to create deep ponds for protection. Using branches, mud, and stones, they (3)______ lodges with underwater entrances, keeping predators away. These dams also help control flooding and (4)______wetlands that support various species. Beavers' building skills shape entire ecosystems, making them one of nature’s (5)______influential beings. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: d The sentence is describing the location of the dams that beavers build, specifically in relation to rivers. The word "across" indicates that the beavers are building their dams spanning the width of the rivers. This is the typical way beavers construct their dams — they build across rivers to block the flow of water and create ponds. "Above" would suggest that the dams are built over the rivers, which is not typical for beaver dams. "Behind" would imply that the dams are located on the opposite side of the river or further back, which doesn't accurately describe how beaver dams work. "Beside" would imply that the dams are next to the rivers, but it doesn’t capture the idea of crossing over the river to block it. Therefore, "across" is the correct choice because it best describes how beavers build their dams from one side of the river to the other.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Beavers are large, semi-aquatic rodents (1)______ in North America and Europe, known for their sharp teeth and powerful tails. They are expert engineers, building dams (2)_______rivers to create deep ponds for protection. Using branches, mud, and stones, they (3)______ lodges with underwater entrances, keeping predators away. These dams also help control flooding and (4)______wetlands that support various species. Beavers' building skills shape entire ecosystems, making them one of nature’s (5)______influential beings. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: a The passage describes beavers building lodges, which requires the act of constructing or creating something from materials. The word "construct" means to build or assemble, making it the best fit in this context. "Damages" means to cause harm or destruction, which is the opposite of what beavers are doing; they are building, not damaging. "Vitiates" means to spoil or impair the quality or efficiency of something, which is also incorrect in this context, as it does not align with the idea of building or creating lodges. "Procures" means to obtain or acquire, which doesn't make sense here because beavers are not obtaining lodges; they are building them. Therefore, "construct" is the correct choice because it fits the idea of beavers actively building their lodges.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Beavers are large, semi-aquatic rodents (1)______ in North America and Europe, known for their sharp teeth and powerful tails. They are expert engineers, building dams (2)_______rivers to create deep ponds for protection. Using branches, mud, and stones, they (3)______ lodges with underwater entrances, keeping predators away. These dams also help control flooding and create wetlands (4)______ support various species. Beavers' building skills shape entire ecosystems, making them one of nature’s (5)______influential beings. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Which
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} That
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Who
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} img {max-width:100% !important} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b In this sentence, the relative pronoun is used to introduce a non-defining relative clause that gives additional information about the wetlands. Since the word "wetlands" is a non-human noun, the correct relative pronoun to use is "which". "Whom" is used for people, so it is incorrect here since the sentence refers to wetlands, not people. "That" could be used in defining relative clauses, but in this case, the clause provides extra information, making "which" the better choice for a non-defining relative clause. "Who" is used for people, so it’s incorrect in this context because we are talking about wetlands, not people. Therefore, "which" is the correct choice because it refers to non-human things (wetlands) and introduces a non-defining relative clause.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Beavers are large, semi-aquatic rodents (1)______ in North America and Europe, known for their sharp teeth and powerful tails. They are expert engineers, building dams (2)_______rivers to create deep ponds for protection. Using branches, mud, and stones, they (3)______ lodges with underwater entrances, keeping predators away. These dams also help control flooding and (4)______wetlands that support various species. Beavers' building skills shape entire ecosystems, making them one of nature’s (5)______influential beings. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b The sentence is describing beavers as one of the most influential beings in nature, which implies they are highly influential among all creatures. The correct word to express this superlative degree is "most". "More" indicates a comparative degree, which would suggest beavers are more influential than some others, but not necessarily the most influential, so it's not appropriate here. "Better" is also a comparative word, used to compare the quality or degree of something, but it doesn't fit when expressing the highest degree of influence. "Best" is typically used to describe the highest quality in a group, but in this context, "most" is more appropriate because we're talking about influence, not quality. Therefore, "most" fits best to indicate that beavers are among the most influential beings in nature.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Convert the sentence into direct speech: He said that he had visited the museum the previous day.
options: He said, "I visited the museum yesterday."
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} He said, "I am visiting the museum tomorrow."
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} He said, "I visit the museum every day."
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} He said, "I was visiting the museum yesterday."
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: a Explanation: The given indirect speech sentence, "He said that he had visited the museum the previous day," needs to be converted into direct speech. "Had visited" changes to "visited". "The previous day" reverts to "yesterday" in direct speech. Hence, the direct speech sentence is: "He said, 'I visited the museum yesterday.'"

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. I wrote to Giordano this morning, / and when we return to Paris / all will have been being arranged.
options: and when we return to Paris
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} all will have been being arranged.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} I wrote to Giordano this morning,
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: b Explanation: The phrase "will have been being arranged" is grammatically incorrect and unnecessarily complex. The correct construction would be "all will have been arranged,"which uses the appropriate future perfect tense without the redundancy of "being."
